medical_information.past_medical_history: Generally healthy 13-year-old male with no history of hospitalizations or surgeries. Full-term birth without complications per parent report. Immunizations up to date through pediatrician. History of mild seasonal allergic rhinitis (spring and early fall), managed with over-the-counter antihistamines as needed, no prior need for emergency care. Had a minor left wrist sprain playing soccer at age 10; treated with a brace for two weeks, no fracture, full recovery without ongoing issues. No history of asthma, diabetes, seizures, heart problems, or concussions. No known history of COVID-19 complications; had a mild COVID-19 infection in late 2022, recovered at home in about a week with rest and fluids, no lingering symptoms. No known mental health diagnoses; parents note occasional test-related anxiety but nothing that has required formal treatment. No history of fainting with exercise or chest pain during physical activity. Cleared for school sports by pediatrician at most recent well visit in 2024.
medical_information.other_pertinent_history: Participant lives with both parents and a younger sister in a non-smoking household. Family keeps a dog at home (Labrador mix); youth has no pet allergies. Family history: paternal grandfather has type 2 diabetes and hypertension; maternal grandmother has seasonal allergies and mild asthma. No family history of sudden cardiac death before age 40, no known hereditary heart rhythm disorders, and no known bleeding disorders. Ethan is right-handed and plays recreational soccer and basketball; he is accustomed to regular physical activity but may need reminders to stay hydrated in hot weather, as he tends to get caught up in activities and forget to drink water. He wears corrective eyeglasses for nearsightedness (mild myopia) and should ideally have them available for any classroom-based or detailed visual activities, but he can participate safely in physical drills without them. No history of behavioral issues or aggression; teachers describe him as cooperative but somewhat shy in new groups until he warms up. Parents note that Ethan can become slightly lightheaded if he stands up too quickly after sitting for a long time, but this has never resulted in a fall or loss of consciousness; he has been advised simply to stand up more slowly.
medical_information.allergies: Seasonal environmental allergies: pollen (tree and grass) causing mild nasal congestion and sneezing in spring and early fall. No known medication allergies. No known food allergies. No history of anaphylaxis. No known insect-sting allergy beyond normal local swelling and redness.
medical_information.medications: Takes cetirizine (Zyrtec) 10 mg by mouth once daily as needed during peak allergy seasons (typically April–June and September–October). Often not taken on days when symptoms are mild or absent. Uses saline nasal spray as needed for congestion (over the counter). No other prescription medications. No routine use of inhalers, steroids, or controlled substances. Parents provide all medications and supervise dosing.
medical_information.health_insurance_carrier: Horizon Blue Cross Blue Shield of New Jersey
